zh.lifeSummary: 當Leticia Joan Corbin於1415年出生在英國肯特麥德斯同時，她的父親William Corbin36歲，母親Elizabeth Margery Blount36歲。她於1432年與Lord Robert Lee Fisher I在英國英格蘭肯特麥德斯同結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1477年8月1日在她的家鄉享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭希迪 本 倫敦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Leticia Joan Corbin于1415年出生在英国肯特麥德斯同时，她的父亲William Corbin36岁，母亲Elizabeth Margery Blount36岁。她于1432年与Lord Robert Lee Fisher I在英国英格兰肯特麥德斯同结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1477年8月1日在她的家乡享年62岁，埋葬在英国英格兰希迪 本 倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Corbin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French and English: nickname meaning ‘little crow, raven’, possibly applied to a black haired man, from Old French, Middle English corbin, a diminutive of corb ‘raven’ (Anglo-Norman French corbin, corfbin ‘crow, raven’).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of the places in Calvados and Orne, France, named Corbon.

variants.name: Carbin
meanings: Swedish: soldier's name, from Swedish karbin ‘carbine’, a type of short rifle or musket used by cavalry.
Cornish: hypercorrect backformation from Cornish carr-bons ‘causeway’, or a habitational name from Carbis in Lelant (Cornwall), of the same derivation.

variants.name: Corvin
meanings: English (Durham): possibly a variant of   Corbin  .
Altered form of Scottish, Irish or English   Colvin  .
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Korving (mainly The Hague): from the personal name Korving, from Corvinus, from Latin corvus ‘raven’.

variants.name: Corban
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Ó Corbáin ‘descendant of Corbán’ or Ó Coirbín ‘descendant of Coirbín’, both possibly from an occupational name based on corb ‘chariot’. Both names have also been Anglicized as Corbett .
Irish: alternatively, a variant of Currivan, from Ó Coirbheáin, itself a variant of Corban (pronounced with three syllables in Irish).
Romanian: nickname derived from corb ‘crow’, with the augmentative suffix -an.

nameMeanings.name: Leticia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin personal name Laetitia; see Lettice .

variants.name: Laetitia
meanings: Pronounced ‘le-tish-a’: Latin form of Lettice . This form has now been largely superseded by the various modern simplified spellings, which are particularly popular among Blacks in the English-speaking world.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Leticia Joan Corbin was born in 1415, in Maidstone, Kent, England, her father, William Corbin, was 36 and her mother, Elizabeth Margery Blount, was 36. She married Lord Robert Lee Fisher I in 1432, in Maidstone, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died on 1 August 1477, in her hometown, at the age of 62, and was buried in City of London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Leticia Joan Corbin est née en 1415, à Maidstone, Kent, Angleterre, son père, William Corbin, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Margery Blount, avait 36 ans. Elle a épousé Lord Robert Lee Fisher I en 1432, à Maidstone, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée le 1 août 1477, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à City of London, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Lei sposato Lord Robert Lee Fisher I nel 1432, nel Maidstone, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. ja.lifeSummary: Leticia Joan Corbinが1415年にイギリスケントメイドストーンで生まれた時，父William Corbinは36歳，母Elizabeth Margery Blountは36歳であった。1432年にイギリスイギリスケントメイドストーンでLord Robert Lee Fisher Iと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1477年8月1日に故郷で62歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスシトイ オフ ロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Leticia Joan Corbin(이)가 1415년 영국 켄트 메이드스톤에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Corbin(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Margery Blount(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1432년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 메이드스톤에서 Lord Robert Lee Fisher I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1477년 8월 1일 그녀의 고향에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 시티 프 런던에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Leticia Joan Corbin родилась в 1415 г. в местности меидстоун, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени William Corbin было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Margery Blount, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lord Robert Lee Fisher I в 1432 г. в местности меидстоун, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла 1 августа 1477 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности сити оф лондон, Англия, Великобритания.
